title: rumex acetosa subsp. nebroides
intro: Rumex acetosa subsp. nebroides, commonly known as Sorrel or Red Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buckwheat family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, to treat conditions such as diarrhea, sore throats, and fever due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, iron, and potassium, which offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, as well as support for immune function and cardiovascular health. Rumex acetosa subsp. nebroides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a culinary herb in salads and soups to add a tangy flavor.
